turns.content: I'm writing a library and I keep getting confused about when to use #[doc = "..."] versus /// comments. Is it better to just put the doc string directly in the attribute so rustc can parse it automatically, or should I rely on the inline comment syntax for everything?

For a hobbyist project like yours, #[doc = "..."] is generally preferred because it keeps your documentation in one place and makes it easier to update. The /// syntax works fine too but #[doc] lets you add extra attributes like #[doc(hidden)] or #[doc(alias = "...")] that are impossible with comments alone. If you need the doc to appear inline when re-exporting a type, use #[doc(inline)], otherwise just keep everything in the attribute block for consistency.
